description:
You are acting as a technical writer who needs a quick, reproducible overview of all unfinished work that is marked with the word “TODO” inside the documentation project that lives in:

/home/user/docs

Sub-directories already contain a few Markdown files.  
Your job is to scan every “*.md” file under /home/user/docs (recursively), capture each line that contains the literal string “TODO” (case–sensitive), and write the findings to a new log file called

/home/user/docs/todo_summary.log

VERY IMPORTANT – the log file must follow **exactly** the format below because automated grading relies on it:

1. Each matching line must be written on its own line in the log.
2. Each line must have three colon-separated fields in this order:
   RELATIVE_PATH:LINE_NUMBER:LINE_CONTENT
   • RELATIVE_PATH is the path of the file relative to /home/user/docs with no leading “./”.  
   • LINE_NUMBER is the 1-based line number where the TODO appears.  
   • LINE_CONTENT is the full, unmodified text of that line (including any additional text after “TODO” but excluding the trailing newline).
3. After writing all matching lines, append a final summary line of the form  
   TOTAL: N  
   where N is the total count of TODO lines written above.
4. Sort the TODO lines alphabetically by RELATIVE_PATH, and when two TODOs come from the same file, sort those by LINE_NUMBER in ascending order.
5. Ensure the entire file ends with a single newline (including after the TOTAL line).

You have two additional constraints:

• Use a Unix-style command pipeline that involves no more than three simple commands strung together by pipes or redirections.  
• Make the pipeline resilient: if any component of the pipeline returns a non-zero exit status (for example, if no “*.md” files are found in a sub-directory), the overall workflow must still finish and produce the correct log file rather than aborting mid-stream.

Once you believe the task is complete, you can open the log file to visually confirm its contents, but the automated tests will be the final judge. No other output files should be created.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and file contents before the agent starts:

/home/user/docs
└── chapter1
    ├── introduction.md
    └── setup.md
└── chapter2
    └── advanced-topics.md

File contents (with 1-based line numbers):

/home/user/docs/chapter1/introduction.md
1  # Introduction
2  Overview of the project.
3  TODO: add more detail about scope
4  Additional background text.
5  TODO - revise later

/home/user/docs/chapter1/setup.md
1  # Setup
2  Prerequisites
3  Build instructions.
4  TODO : include installation steps

/home/user/docs/chapter2/advanced-topics.md
1  # Advanced Topics
2  TODO: add performance tips
3  TODO - add case studies
4  Completed first draft.

The correct todo_summary.log that the agent must produce:

chapter1/introduction.md:3:TODO: add more detail about scope
chapter1/introduction.md:5:TODO - revise later
chapter1/setup.md:4:TODO : include installation steps
chapter2/advanced-topics.md:2:TODO: add performance tips
chapter2/advanced-topics.md:3:TODO - add case studies
TOTAL: 5
␊  (␊ indicates the required single trailing newline)

NB: The trailing newline must be present after the “TOTAL: 5” line.

Automated tests will:

1. Verify the todo_summary.log file exists at /home/user/docs/todo_summary.log.
2. Read its contents and compare **exactly** with the block shown above, including order, spacing, colons and the terminating newline.
3. Confirm no extra files were created anywhere under /home/user/docs or the working directory.

Permissions: the entire /home/user/docs tree and anything created beneath it are writable by the unprivileged user running the agent.
